raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general request to review a research section centrally includes checking whether the argument from problem statement to method claim to evaluation conclusion is coherent.","R1.3":"The section reports empirical coverage and interval-width results, so review responsibility includes checking whether the stated claims are aligned with the reported evidence.","R1.4":"The artifact makes novelty claims such as being the first procedure and a step beyond prior CATE inference tools, making novelty assessment central.","R1.6":"Reviewing a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, concision, and readability.","R1.7":"The section contains an explicit related-work paragraph and attribution claims, so citation coverage and scholarship are central to the review.","RX.1":"The projection should preserve uncertainty about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The review scope must avoid endorsing broad firstness or default-method claims beyond the available artifact.","RX.3":"The agent must project responsibilities only and not perform the requested review.","RX.4":"The projection should account for risks from unsupported causal-inference claims influencing downstream scientific conclusions.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact, such as the related-work and evaluation claims."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general request to review a research section centrally includes checking whether the argument from problem statement to method claim to evaluation conclusion is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports empirical coverage and interval-width results, so review responsibility includes checking whether the stated claims are aligned with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes novelty claims such as being the first procedure and a step beyond prior CATE inference tools, making novelty assessment central.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, concision, and readability.
rationale.R1.7: The section contains an explicit related-work paragraph and attribution claims, so citation coverage and scholarship are central to the review.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review scope must avoid endorsing broad firstness or default-method claims beyond the available artifact.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must project responsibilities only and not perform the requested review.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for risks from unsupported causal-inference claims influencing downstream scientific conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact, such as the related-work and evaluation claims.
